response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong testing discipline, GIS platform experience (GISCollective), and low-latency systems work at Sociomantic — but the role specifically requires modern C++ or Rust expertise, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (Dec 2014–Jun 2015) over a decade ago. Primary languages have since been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. The geospatial background and willingness to work across the stack are genuine pluses, but the core C++/Rust requirement is a significant gap that would likely require ramp-up.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience via GISCollective, directly relevant to routing tiles
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling authorship
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of req/sec, async I/O, fibers)
concerns: Only ~6 months of C/C++ professional experience, over 10 years ago; no recent modern C++ or Rust
No demonstrated experience with navigation/routing systems or map data pipelines specifically
Recent career trajectory is web/TypeScript/Node-focused, distant from systems-level C++ work

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent modern C++ / Rust experience
